description: Xero Accounting API integration. Reads contacts, invoices, accounts, payments, bank transactions, items, credit notes, the organisation, and financial reports; creates and updates contacts, invoices, and payments. Multi-tenant: every action except list_connections takes a tenant_id (discover ids via list_connections). Created invoices default to DRAFT and create actions accept an idempotency key. Authenticated with a user-authorized Xero OAuth2 token presented as a Bearer header against api.xero.com, with the Xero-tenant-id header set per call.

oauth.pending_instructions: Register an app at https://developer.xero.com/app/manage (Web app). Set the redirect URI to http://localhost:9876/callback, copy the Client ID and Client Secret into XERO_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and XERO_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET on the IronClaw host, then run `ironclaw tool auth xero` to launch the consent flow. The host must request offline_access so it can refresh the 30-minute access token (refresh tokens last 60 days and rotate on each use). The granular accounting.* scopes above were mandatory for new apps from 2 March 2026; confirm the exact scope names for the operations you enable against https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes/ before going live.

auth.instructions:
1. Sign in at https://developer.xero.com/app/manage and click New app. Choose Web app, name it (e.g. IronClaw Xero Tool), set the company URL, and set the OAuth 2.0 redirect URI to http://localhost:9876/callback.
2. Open the app's Configuration, copy the Client ID, then Generate a secret and copy it.
3. On the IronClaw host export both values:

   ```sh
   export XERO_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=<client id>
   export XERO_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=<client secret>
   ```

4. Run `ironclaw tool auth xero` to complete the consent flow. Approve the organisation(s) you want the tool to reach; a user can grant access to several.
5. After auth, call the tool's list_connections action to get the tenant id for each authorized organisation, then pass tenant_id on every other action.
6. Access tokens expire after 30 minutes; the host refreshes them using the refresh token (valid 60 days, rotated on each refresh) granted by the offline_access scope. Re-authenticate only if the user revokes the connection.
auth.notes: Create and update actions are write operations against live financial records. Invoices are created as DRAFT unless an explicit Status is supplied. Pass an idempotency_key on create actions so a retried call does not post a duplicate invoice or payment.
